perturbation.original_text: Power2Ship must provide WLI with a weekly report listing the serial number of every Unit shipped to a customer during the prior week.
perturbation.changed_text: Power2Ship should endeavor to provide WLI with a periodic report listing the serial number of Units shipped to a customer.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'must provide' to 'should endeavor to provide' weakens the obligation. Replacing 'weekly' with 'periodic' introduces ambiguity about the frequency of reporting. This makes it difficult to enforce consistent and timely reporting.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Failure to provide accurate and timely reports on shipped units can hinder compliance with inventory and sales tax regulations.
perturbation.law_explanation: N.J.A.C. 18:24-6.1 requires businesses to keep detailed records of sales, including dates, item descriptions, and amounts. By making the reporting of serial numbers periodic and discretionary, Power2Ship may fail to maintain adequate records, potentially violating New Jersey sales tax regulations.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires a 'weekly report,' while the changed text suggests a 'periodic report,' introducing ambiguity regarding the frequency of reporting. Additionally, the original text states Power2Ship 'must provide', whereas the changed text states Power2Ship 'should endeavor to provide,' weakening the obligation. Although the law snippet is unavailable, the perturbation explanation details that this could result in non-compliance with regulatory requirements, leading to a legal contradiction under the definition of Ambiguity Legal Contradiction, which states that a legal contradiction happens when an obligation is introduced ambiguously, making it difficult to enforce under state or national law. This change makes enforcement difficult as the term 'periodic' is not clearly defined, allowing Power2Ship to potentially delay reports, creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Power2Ship must provide a separate weekly report to WLI that lists the serial number of any Units suspended during the prior week and that includes the SIM # and ID, date of return, name of returning customer and reason for return.
perturbation.changed_text: Power2Ship will attempt to provide a report, when feasible, listing serial numbers of suspended Units with available SIM # and ID, date of return, and reason for return.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'must provide' to 'will attempt to provide, when feasible' weakens the obligation, making it conditional. Removing 'weekly' and adding 'available' introduces ambiguity. This undermines WLI's ability to track deactivated units which can affect auditing requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Compromises the ability to accurately report and reconcile sales, potentially violating sales tax laws and creating auditing difficulties.
perturbation.law_explanation: N.J.A.C. 18:24-6.3 requires that business records must clearly reflect all sales and use tax transactions. By making the reporting of suspended units discretionary and incomplete, Power2Ship may fail to maintain records that accurately reflect all sales tax transactions, potentially violating New Jersey sales tax regulations.
perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates a 'weekly report' with specific details, while the changed text makes the report discretionary ('will attempt, when feasible') and incomplete ('available SIM # and ID'). The Law Explanation states this change may violate N.J.A.C. 18:24-6.3, which requires business records to clearly reflect all sales and use tax transactions. The changed text introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation to provide a complete and timely report, potentially hindering the ability to maintain accurate sales tax records. This falls under the 'Ambiguities Legal Contradiction' definition, where an obligation is introduced ambiguously, potentially leading to non-compliance and disputes.

perturbation.original_text: Power2Ship must provide WLI with a complete copy of its monthly airtime bill for the Units as received by the respective wireless operators (T-Mobile, Verizon, AT&T, etc.). Power2Ship is required to keep all the WLI Unit activations on every wireless network on a separate account from any other vendor's brand used by Power2Ship. Every such monthly invoice (one per operator) must be submitted to WLI within 7 days from the date it is received by Power2Ship and the invoice must include all pages listed on the invoice (example 7of 7 pages). Power2Ship's Chief Executive Officer or Chief Financial Officer must confirm in writing to WLI every quarter that the account numbers under which the WLI Units are being activated on the various networks are the only accounts under which the WLI units are activated.
perturbation.changed_text: Power2Ship will endeavor to provide WLI with a copy of its airtime bill for the Units. Power2Ship will try to keep WLI Unit activations on a separate account, resources permitting. The monthly invoice should be submitted to WLI within a reasonable timeframe. Power2Ship's management should attempt to confirm that the account numbers under which the WLI Units are being activated are the only accounts under which the WLI units are activated.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'must provide' to 'will endeavor to provide' and 'is required to keep' to 'will try to keep' weakens obligations, making them conditional. Replacing 'within 7 days' with 'within a reasonable timeframe' introduces ambiguity. Removing the requirement for a quarterly confirmation from the CEO or CFO further weakens the obligation to verify account separation. This significantly impacts the auditing abilities of sales.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Compromises WLI's ability to verify royalty payments and potential violation of revenue laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: 26 U.S. Code § 6001 requires every person liable for any tax to keep records that accurately reflect their income and deductions. By weakening the requirement for accurate and timely reporting of airtime bills and account separations, Power2Ship could hinder WLI's ability to accurately determine and report its revenue, potentially violating federal tax laws.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 26 U.S. Code § 6001 - Notice or regulations requiring records, statements, and special returns | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 26 U.S. Code § 6001 - Notice or regulations requiring records, statements, and special returns U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) Every person liable for any tax imposed by this title, or for the collection thereof, shall keep such records, render such statements, make such returns, and comply with such rules and regulations as the Secretary may from time to time prescribe. Whenever in the judgment of the Secretary it is necessary, he may require any person, by notice served upon such person or by regulations, to make such returns, render such statements, or keep such records, as the Secretary deems sufficient to show whether or not such person is liable for tax under this title. The only records which an employer shall be required to keep under this section in connection with charged tips shall be charge receipts, records necessary to comply with section 6053(c), and copies of statements furnished by employees under section 6053(a). (Aug. 16, 1954, ch. 736, 68A Stat. 731 ; Pub. L. 94–455, title XIX, § 1906(b)(13)(A) , Oct. 4, 1976 , 90 Stat. 1834 ; Pub. L. 95–600, title V, § 501(a) , Nov. 6, 1978 , 92 Stat. 2878 ; Pub. L. 97–248, title III, § 314(d) , Sept. 3, 1982 , 96 Stat. 605 .) Editorial Notes Amendments 1982— Pub. L. 97–248 inserted “, records necessary to comply with section 6053(c),” after “charge receipts”. 1978— Pub. L. 95–600 inserted provision at end relating to only records which an employer shall be required to keep in connection with charged tips. 1976— Pub. L. 94–455 struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary” wherever appearing. Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Effective Date of 1982 Amendment Amendment by Pub. L. 97–248 applicable to calendar years beginning after Dec. 31, 1982 , see section 314(e) of Pub. L. 97–248
perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates specific actions (e.g., 'must provide', 'is required to keep', 'within 7 days'), whereas the changed text introduces ambiguity and weakens these obligations (e.g., 'will endeavor to provide', 'will try to keep', 'within a reasonable timeframe'). The Law Explanation states that weakening these requirements could hinder WLI's ability to accurately determine and report its revenue, potentially violating federal tax laws under 26 U.S. Code § 6001. Scraped Law Snippet 2 states that 'Every person liable for any tax imposed by this title...shall keep such records...as the Secretary may from time to time prescribe.' The changed text introduces ambiguity into the record-keeping and reporting process, directly undermining the clarity and enforceability of this legal requirement and thus creating a contradiction.
